rulings.Case Title: CHERYL GROB ET AL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 3, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Intervenors Massachusetts Bay Insurance Company, et al.'s (insurers of Marconi Plastering Company, Inc.) general demurrer is sustained without leave to amend pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e) for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. Intervenors' request for judicial notice is granted. No opposition filed and no showing made that the statute of limitations bar is capable of amendment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE STROTHERS VS. LAMONS GASKET COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 3, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary adjudication. The Court received and reviewed supplemental briefs submitted by each side on the motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages. Defendant's objection to the Court considering Plaintiff's Exhibit D (Plaintiff's Supplemental Responses to Defendant's Interrogatories, dated September 1, 2017) is sustained. Plaintiff's Exhibit D was not cited to in Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in Opposition to Defendant's motion for Summary Adjudication of punitive damages as required by CCP §437c(b)(3). Plaintiff may not rely on Exhibit D in opposition to the motion. As to summary adjudication of Plaintiff's claim for punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's Exhibits S (Deposition of Holthouser) and V (Deposition of Demarse) create a triable issue on punitive damages. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
